evidence_excerpt: Multiple embolizations allowed cure of the vein of Galen malformation; at 4.5 years she remained somewhat delayed in motor, speech, and cognition but could follow normal day-care activities and her large head circumference (+3 SD) had stabilized.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Residual issues included a large head circumference (+3 SD) that stabilized over time and ongoing developmental delay; no ongoing acute symptoms were reported.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: A few days after diagnosis, first endovascular embolization of two branches was performed with acrylic glue.
Successful acute ventriculocisternostomy for hydrocephalus produced a good clinical result.
After initial embolization, clinical development was favourable with improvements in all aspects.
Further embolization at 12 months of age as part of staged treatment.
Additional embolizations at 20 months and 2.5 years; total of four interventional sessions.
Final imaging showed cure of the vein of Galen malformation; head circumference stabilized.

notes_on_uncertainty: This case report includes three different patients. The extraction above pertains to Case 1 (girl with vein of Galen malformation). Index time was taken as the admission when MRI confirmed the malformation and treatment planning began at 6 months of age. Exact dates are not provided; ages were used to approximate short-, mid-, and long-term periods.
